description: Golden manifest for the ticketing fixture (the 'gatekeep' event-ticketing service, written in TypeScript / NestJS + TypeORM). Defines what a faithful distillation of harness/fixtures/ticketing/codebase must capture (entities, states, transitions, rules, config) and what it must NOT contain (exclusions: implementation details and dead code planted as traps). This fixture exists to prove the distiller generalizes beyond Python.
allowed_extra_entities_note: Entities a faithful spec may legitimately introduce beyond the manifest's domain entities: the organizer/scanner actors, and cross-cutting communication entities the patterns docs say to model as external/shared. Any model entity NOT here and NOT an expected entity is flagged as a possible confabulation (review signal, not auto-fail). The scorer treats each manifest entity's state and transition lists as EXHAUSTIVE, so an extra state or edge on a matched entity is auto-failed.

Event.note: sold_out -> on_sale is the capacity-release reopen (EventsService.releaseCapacity). Event.markCancelled() blocks only closed/cancelled, so cancel is reachable from draft, on_sale AND sold_out — a cancel-from-any-non-terminal wildcard. on_sale -> cancelled is the common path (required); draft/sold_out -> cancelled are allowed (optional) so a distiller that reads the markCancelled guard is not flagged for confabulation.

WaitlistEntry.note: Implicit state machine in code: derived from nullable timestamps (joined_at, offered_at, claimed_at, expired_at) on WaitlistEntry, exposed via isPending/isOffered/isClaimed/isExpired getters. The code defines NO state-name strings, so the distiller must invent names; state_aliases lists accepted synonyms. Grow this list only for genuinely equivalent names, never to excuse a missing state.

Attendee.note: Synced from an external identity provider via webhook (IdentityWebhookController); the system reads but never originates attendee records.

PaymentIntent.note: Mirror of a charge at the external payment provider. gatekeep originates it only in 'created'; the provider drives it to succeeded/failed via webhook. created -> succeeded ; created -> failed. Terminal: succeeded, failed.

rules.description: Attendee places an order (OrdersService.createOrder): Order created in 'pending'.
Attendee joins an event's waitlist (WaitlistService.join): WaitlistEntry created in 'pending'.
Reserve a ticket against an event for an order: requires the event on_sale with remaining capacity, holds one unit of inventory and creates a Ticket in 'reserved' (TicketsService.reserve).

Payment intent succeeds: the backing order is confirmed and its reserved tickets move to paid (OrdersService.confirmFromPayment, driven by the payment webhook). 'succeeded' fingerprints the payment-provider trigger.
Fulfil a confirmed order: its paid tickets are issued and emailed to the attendee, and the order moves to fulfilled (OrdersService.fulfil + TicketsService.issueOrderTickets).
Scanner checks a ticket in at the gate: requires an issued ticket whose event is not cancelled; issued -> checked_in (GateService.checkIn).
Refund a paid or issued ticket: ticket -> refunded, the payment intent is reversed at the provider, the held seat is released and the attendee notified (TicketsService.refund).
Cancel a still-pending order: its reserved tickets expire, their held capacity is released, order -> cancelled (OrdersService.cancel).

Organizer cancels an event: event -> cancelled and EVERY paid/issued ticket is refunded with its payment intent reversed. The cascade is deliberately scattered across the controller (orchestration), the entity (Event.markCancelled flips status) and the service (EventsService.refundAllTicketsForCancelledEvent does the refunds).
When capacity frees up on an event, the oldest pending waitlist entry is offered the seat (offered_at + offer_expires_at stamped) and emailed: pending -> offered (WaitlistService.offerNextForEvent).
Attendee claims an outstanding waitlist offer: the entry is marked claimed and a ticket is reserved for them via a fresh order: offered -> claimed plus a Ticket in 'reserved' (WaitlistService.claim).

Scheduled job: unpaid reservations whose hold window has elapsed expire and their seats are released. Re-fire guard: only tickets still 'reserved' with an elapsed hold. reserved -> expired (ScheduledService.expireUnpaidReservations).

Scheduled job: waitlist offers unclaimed past their offer window expire. Re-fire guard: only entries still offered (offered_at set, claimed/expired null). offered -> expired (ScheduledService.expireUnclaimedOffers).

Scheduled job: on_sale events whose remaining capacity has reached zero are marked sold_out. Re-fire guard in markSoldOutIfFull no-ops unless still on_sale with zero capacity. on_sale -> sold_out (ScheduledService.markSoldOutEvents).
Scheduled job: sold-out events whose end time has passed are closed. Re-fire guard: only events still 'sold_out' with ends_at in the past. sold_out -> closed (ScheduledService.closeEndedEvents).
Identity-provider webhook upserts the local attendee mirror. Fingerprinted on the attendee external entity so it is not confused with other inbound webhooks (IdentityWebhookController.sync).
Payment-provider webhook reports a failed charge: the payment intent moves created -> failed and the order stays pending (PaymentWebhookController, failure branch).

boundaries_note: System boundaries that a faithful distillation should expose as surfaces / actors (skill step 5.5: actors from auth patterns). Matched fuzzily against the spec's `surface`, `facing`, `actor` and `external entity` declarations by alias substring, since surface naming and granularity vary across runs. Reported as boundary_recall; informational, not gated.

exclusions.description: Terms that must NOT appear in a faithful spec. Dead-code traps planted in the fixture and implementation details the skill instructs to abstract away.
